UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO GEORGE ENG ONLY AS TO 14TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ALICE EUBANK DEPOSITION P. 216, LN. 22-25. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE #1 (CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 3, 8, 9, AND 10) - ISSUES RAISED BY A SUMMARY JUDGMENT MOTION ARE FRAMED BY THE PLEADINGS. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO MEET ITS INITIAL BURDEN AND TO COMPLETELY DISPOSE OF A CAUSE OF ACTION. CCP 437C(F)(1). THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT BASED SOLELY ON ALLEGED CONTRACT TO CONVEY OWNERSHIP. SEE 31, 35, 66, 22, ETC. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE #2 (CAUSES OF ACTION 2, 8, AND 1)- TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO BREACH OF 2005 CONTRACT. ALICE EUBANK DEPOSITION P. 78, LN. 8-21. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE # 3 (CAUSES OF ACTION 6, 8, 9, 10, 14, AND 15) - TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO THE DATE OF STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ACCRUAL. 2010 WHEN ALICE EUBANK WAS ALLEGEDLY FORCED TO STOP PERFORMING HER DUTIES UNDER 2005 AGREEMENT OR 2013 WHEN PROPERTY WAS CONVEYED TO JIMMY ENG. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO ISSUE #5 (CAUSES OF ACTION 14 AND 15), EXCEPT AS INDICATED ABOVE - TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO EXTREME EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK B. PAREDES, REPORTER MARK I. BRICKMAN, ESQ. CSR. 5527 (501)

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANT ALICE EUBANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. FIRST, FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION, MS. EUBANKS HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS EACH CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE SHE FAILS TO ADDRESS HER ALLEGED ACTS OF CONVERSION AND FRAUD IN MAY AND JUNE 2011. (COMPLAINT, 18, 22 - 24.) PLAINTIFF GOOEY "GEORGE" OCK ENG FILED THIS ACTION ON MAY 17, 2013, WHICH IS WITHIN TWO YEARS OF THESE ALLEGED ACTS. SECOND, FOR FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, MOVING PARTY HAS NOT PROFFERED MATERIAL FACTS IN SUPPORT OF ALL OF THE GROUNDS STATED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION. (NOTICE OF MOTION, P. 2:12-14, SEE UMF NOS. 1 - 20.) THIRD, FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, DEFENDANT FAILS TO ADDRESS ALL ALLEGATIONS OF FRAUD AND CONCEALMENT. (COMPLAINT, 39(E).) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK B. PAREDES, REPORTER MARK I. BRICKMAN, CSR. 5527 (501)

Defendant Alice Eubank'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tad Telvin, Esq. of Kaufman, Dolowich & Voluck, LLP (415-926-7635) for Alice Eubank Defendant and moving party.
Seth W. Wiener, Esq. Attorney at law (925-828-8648) for Cross Defendants and moving parties Jimmy Eng and Judy Jong.
Ingrid M. Evans, Esq. Attorney at law (415-441-8669) for "George" Ock Eng Plaintiff and opposing party.
Elliot Wong, Esq. of The Evans Law Firm (415-441-8669) for "George" Ock Eng Plaintiff and opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties the Court's tentative Ruling Stands as follows; Defendant Alice Eubank'S Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively Summary Adjudication
is DENIED. Defendant has not demonstrated that there are no triable issues of fact. First, for each cause of action, Ms. Eubanks has not demonstrated that the statute of limitations bars each cause of action because she fails to address her alleged acts of conversion and fraud in May and June 2011. (Complaint, 18, 22 - 24.) Plaintiff Gooey "George" Ock Eng filed this action on May 17, 2013, which is within two years of these alleged acts. Second, for first cause of action, moving party has not proffered material facts in support of all of the grounds stated in the Notice of Motion. (Notice of Motion, p. 2:12-14, See UMF nos. 1 - 20.) Third, for the second cause of action, Defendant fails to address all allegations of fraud and concealment. (Complaint, 39(e).) Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk B. Paredes, Reporter Mark I. Brickman, CSR. 5527 (501)

Cross Defendants Jimmy Eng And Judy Jong'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Tad Telvin, Esq. of Kaufman, Dolowich & Voluck, LLP (415-926-7635) for Alice Eubank Defendant and moving party.
Seth W. Wiener, Esq. Attorney at law (925-828-8648) for Cross Defendants and moving parties Jimmy Eng and Judy Jong.
Ingrid M. Evans, Esq. Attorney at law (415-441-8669) for "George" Ock Eng Plaintiff and opposing party.
Elliot Wong, Esq. of The Evans Law Firm (415-441-8669) for "George" Ock Eng Plaintiff and opposing party.

After hearing arguments from both parties; the Court Adopts the Tentative ruling as follows: Cross Defendants Jimmy Eng And Judy Jong'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to George Eng only as to 14th cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Alice Eubank Deposition p. 216, ln. 22-25.
Motion is DENIED as to Issue #1 (causes of action 2, 3, 8, 9, and 10) - Issues raised by a summary judgment motion are framed by the pleadings. Moving party failed to meet its initial burden and to completely dispose of a cause of action. CCP 437c(f)(1). These causes of action are not based solely on alleged contract to convey ownership. See 31, 35, 66, 22, etc.
Motion is DENIED as to Issue #2 (causes of action 2, 8, and 1)- triable issue of fact as to breach of 2005 contract. Alice Eubank Deposition p. 78, ln. 8-21.
Motion is DENIED as to Issue # 3 (causes of action 6, 8, 9, 10, 14, and 15) - triable issue of fact as to the date of statute of limitations accrual. 2010 when Alice Eubank was allegedly forced to stop performing her duties under 2005 Agreement or 2013 when property was conveyed to Jimmy Eng.
Motion is DENIED as to issue #5 (causes of action 14 and 15), except as indicated above - triable issue of fact as to extreme emotional distress. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk B. Paredes, Reporter Mark I. Brickman, Esq. CSR. 5527 (501)
